content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Karen Mathews, Chief Deputy 
Registrar of Voters 
County of stanislaus 
912 11th st., Room 100 
Modesto, CA 
95354 
Dear Ms. Mathews: 
November 15, 1989 
Re: Combined Campaign Report 
Our File No. 1-89-583 
This is to confirm advice provided to your office on October 
2, 1989, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l This letter constitutes writ-
ten advice under section 84205. 
section 84205 states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements combin-
ing statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
A special election has been called for Assembly District 27. 
The election date is December 5, 1989, and a run-off election is 
to be held on January 30, 1990. 
Because the pre-run-off election 
statement due in connection with the January 30, 1990 election 
(Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for the last 
six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping periods, we 
believe it is appropriate for candidates being voted on in that 
election, their controlled committees, and committees primarily 
formed to support or oppose candidates in that election to combine 
those two reports. 
Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on 
the following schedule: 
1 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322d5660 


Karen Mathews 
Page Two 
~ 
of statement 
1st Pre-Election (for 
Dec. election) 
2nd Pre-Election 
(for Dec. election) 
Pre-run-off Election4 
(for Jan. election) 
Combined with semi-annual 
statement for the second 
half of 1989 
Semi-Annual 
Period covered 
Filing Deadline 
2 
Jan. 1 
- Oct. 21

Nov. 18

Nov. 19 - Jan. 13 
January 18, 1990 
Jan. 14 - June 30 
July 31, 1990 
The contribution limitations imposed by Proposition 73 apply 
separately for a special election cycle and for a special runoff 
election cycle. 
The limitations for each of these cycles are the 
same amounts as the limitations for a fiscal year. 
contributors 
may contribute up to the stated limits (see enclosure) in the 
special election cycle and the special runoff election cycle, less 
any amount during the fiscal year which the candidate transfers to 
his or her special election committee bank account. 5 
In addition, 
contributors may contribute up to the stated limits in the special 
runoff election cycle to candidates being voted upon in the runoff 
election even if they contributed the maximum amounts in the 
fiscal year and in the special election cycle. 
2 
The period covered by any statement begins on the day after 
the closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no 
previous statement has been filed. 
3 
Deadlines which fallon a Saturday, Sunday or official state 
holiday are extended to the next regular business day. 
Therefore, 
the second pre-election statement will be due on November 24, 
1989. 
4 
Candidates who lose the December 5, 1989 election and their 
controlled committees are not required to file the pre-run-off 
election statement due on January 18, 1990. 
Instead, such 
candidates and committees will file a semi-annual statement 
covering November 19 -
December 31, 1989. 
This statement will be 
due no later than January 31, 1990. 
5 
See enclosed proposed temporary order in Service Employees 
International Union, et ale v. Fair Political Practices 
Commission, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of California, 
Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM. 
The court will hold a hearing on 
this issue on November 15, 1989, and issue a permanent order on or 
after that date. 


Karen Mathews 
Page Three 
"special election cycle" means the day on which the office 
becomes vacant until the day of the special election. 
"Special runoff election cycle" means the day after the 
special election until the day of the special runoff election. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. DOnovan 
General couhsel

sections.conclusion: Because the pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the January 30, 1990 election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for candidates being voted on in that election, their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in that election to combine those two reports.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether candidates and committees in a special election with a runoff can combine campaign disclosure reports when reporting periods overlap between the pre-runoff election statement and semi-annual statement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits candidates, their controlled committees, and supporting/opposing committees in the special election to combine the pre-runoff election statement with the semi-annual statement for the second half of 1989 due to overlapping reporting periods. Contribution limitations apply separately for the special election cycle and special runoff election cycle, with contributors able to give up to stated limits in each cycle.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC advice letter confirming oral advice provided October 2, 1989. Contains detailed filing schedule table and contribution limit chart as enclosures. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers, but content clearly structured as advice response under Gov. Code § 84205.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether candidates and committees in a special election with a runoff can combine campaign disclosure reports when reporting periods overlap between the pre-runoff election statement and semi-annual statement.

CONCLUSION: Because the pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the January 30, 1990 election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for candidates being voted on in that election, their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in that election to combine those two reports.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Registrar of Voters for Stanislaus County regarding campaign disclosure requirements for a special Assembly District 27 election held December 5, 1989, with a runoff on January 30, 1990, permitting combined filing of overlapping pre-runoff and semi-annual reports with a detailed filing schedule and contribution limit guidance.
